Politics.headline: Opposition parties grapple with new reality of Liberal majority government
Politics.detail: Following recent elections, opposition parties in Canada are adjusting to the significant power held by the Liberal majority, strategizing how to effectively challenge and influence policy.

Politics.headline: Trump joined by Joe Rogan as he signs order to speed up psychedelic review
Politics.detail: Former President Donald Trump, alongside Joe Rogan, signed an executive order aimed at expediting the review process for potential medical applications of psychedelic drugs.

Politics.headline: Obama and Mamdani meet for the first time.
Politics.detail: Former President Barack Obama met with political strategist and activist Mamdani to discuss strategies to encourage more people to vote in the upcoming election.

Politics.headline: Mandelson scandal is biggest crisis for diplomatic service in decades, says ex-Foreign Office chief
Politics.detail: A former Foreign Office chief stated that the ongoing scandal involving Lord Mandelson's security vetting failure has become the most significant crisis for the UK's diplomatic service in recent decades.

Politics.headline: US judge blocks Justice Department bid to seize voter data in Rhode Island.
Politics.detail: A U.S. judge has prevented the Department of Justice from seizing voter data in Rhode Island, thwarting the federal government's efforts to gain access to sensitive election information.

Finance & Economy.headline: Financial markets fall to new 2026 low as oil rises again
Finance & Economy.detail: Concerns over the ongoing Iran war are contributing to instability in financial markets, driving them to new lows as oil prices surge.

Finance & Economy.headline: Iran war already taking a toll on the global economy, IMF says in new forecast
Finance & Economy.detail: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has cut its 2026 growth estimate, citing the Iran war and an unusual amount of uncertainty clouds the outlook.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gulf Guarantee: Oil plunges after Iran says Strait of Hormuz open to commercial ships.
Finance & Economy.detail: Oil prices experienced a sharp decline following Iran's announcement that the Strait of Hormuz has been reopened for commercial shipping.

Finance & Economy.headline: Markets optimistic after Iran announces Strait of Hormuz has reopened
Finance & Economy.detail: Financial markets are showing optimism following Iran's announcement that the Strait of Hormuz has reopened, potentially easing supply chain concerns.

Finance & Economy.headline: Average new UK electric car price is now lower than petrol vehicles
Finance & Economy.detail: The average price of new electric cars in the UK has fallen below that of petrol vehicles, marking a significant shift in the automotive market.

Technology.headline: Samsung Receives Four Accolades at Prestigious Edison Awards 2026
Technology.detail: Samsung Electronics was honored with two gold and two silver awards at the Edison Awards 2026 for its Smart Modular House, Vision AI Companion, Spatial Signage and Bespoke AI Laundry Combo.

Technology.headline: A Look At Marvell Technology (MRVL) Valuation After Recent Share Price Momentum
Technology.detail: Marvell Technology has shown strong recent share price gains, leading to discussions about its valuation and position as a key player in AI data center infrastructure, with analysts suggesting it is currently undervalued.

Technology.headline: ASX 200 tech shares rocket 13% as long-awaited sector rebound accelerates
Technology.detail: Australian tech shares experienced a significant rebound, with the S&P/ASX 200 Information Technology Index rising nearly 13% last week, driven by gains in companies like WiseTech Global and Xero.

Technology.headline: GeekWire Awards voting is now closed: Thanks for casting ballots to pick the best in Pacific NW tech
Technology.detail: Voting for the GeekWire Awards is now closed, as the Pacific Northwest tech community has cast their ballots to choose the best in the region's technology scene.

Technology.headline: The Osmo Pocket 4 Is the First True Victim of the US Government's DJI Ban
Technology.detail: The DJI Osmo Pocket 4 is affected by the US government's DJI ban.

Science.headline: Dr Thein becomes first Malaysian-born scientist to win 'Science Oscar'
Science.detail: Consultant haematologist Dr Thein Swee Lay has won one of the six 2026 Breakthrough Prizes, also known as the “Oscars of Science,” for discovering the gene B-cell lymphoma/leukaemia 11A (BCL11A), a key regulator of fetal haemoglobin production. Each prize includes a cash award of US$3mil (RM12mil).

Science.headline: Penn, CHOP Clinch Breakthrough Prize for Gene Therapy
Science.detail: Jean Bennett, Albert Maguire, and Katherine High received the Breakthrough Prize in Life Sciences for their work in developing gene therapy for inherited blindness. Their work has been recognized as the “Oscars of Science.”

Science.headline: Trump signs order to accelerate access to psychedelic drug treatments
Science.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Saturday intended to speed up access to medical research and treatment based on psychedelic drugs. The order instructs the FDA to expedite the review of drugs such as ibogaine, which U.S. military veteran groups have said can help treat post-traumatic stress disorder.

Science.headline: Feature: When Portugal meets TCM intelligent robot
Science.detail: An intelligent robot dispensing traditional Chinese medicine (TCM) has debuted at the Instituto Universitario Egas Moniz in Lisbon, Portugal. The robot combines intelligent hardware with a specialized TCM model and advanced AI technologies to offer personalized herbal formulas.

Science.headline: Earth gets brighter every year but progression is volatile, study finds
Science.detail: Scientists have found that the Earth is getting brighter each year, but the progression is volatile. Scientists who analysed nearly 16,000 ancient remains suggest red hair and fair skin is favoured for vitamin D production.

Health.headline: Trump signs order to accelerate access to psychedelic drug treatments
Health.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Saturday intended to speed up access to medical research and treatment based on psychedelic drugs, instructing the FDA to expedite the review of drugs like ibogaine, which some military veteran groups say can help with PTSD. Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. has supported using drugs like ibogaine as alternative treatments for mental health conditions like depression.

Health.headline: Korea-Fiji strengthens its health partnership
Health.detail: Fiji is being urged to maintain health sector investments amid economic pressures, as Korea supports the construction of a National Rehabilitation Centre and early disease detection programs. The project aims to enhance maternal and child health facilities and strengthen community-level health services, focusing on disease prevention and recovery.

Health.headline: No afterthought: Men's health at the fore
Health.detail: The Australian government is engaging with local support groups in the Corangamite region to address men's health issues, including mental health and cancer. The government envoy is visiting the region to connect with local support groups and understand the vital work they do.

Health.headline: Advancing Healthcare Through Global Collaboration
Health.detail: Aga Khan University faculty participated in the Global Ismaili Health Professionals Conference in Houston, focusing on healthcare innovation and global health challenges. Discussions covered AI in healthcare, disease prevention, mental health, climate-related health risks, and ethical decision-making.

Health.headline: Indicators for human exposures to zoonotic pathogens
Health.detail: A WHO-PREZODE collaboration developed standardized indicators to assess the risk of zoonotic disease emergence. The aim is to validate these indicators for assessing and mitigating the risk of diseases that can spread from animals to humans.

Sports.headline: Mitchell scores 32 as Cavaliers defeat Raptors 126-113 in Game 1 of East first-round series
Sports.detail: Donovan Mitchell scored 32 points to lead the Cleveland Cavaliers to a 126-113 victory over the Toronto Raptors in Game 1 of their Eastern Conference first-round series.

Sports.headline: Bayern forward Serge Gnabry likely out for the rest of season, putting World Cup in doubt
Sports.detail: Bayern Munich forward Serge Gnabry is likely to miss the remainder of the season due to injury, casting doubt on his participation in the upcoming World Cup.

Sports.headline: Garret Guillemette hits a grand slam (1) to left field
Sports.detail: Garret Guillemette hit a grand slam to left field in a Minor League Baseball game for the Corpus Christi Hooks.

Sports.headline: We Are Ports TV Highlights | Ballymena Utd 1 Portadown 2 | Sports Direct Premiership
Sports.detail: Highlights from the Sports Direct Premiership match between Ballymena United and Portadown, where Portadown won 2-1.

Sports.headline: Revealed: Pies players' shock claim over Blues star Elijah Hollands as AFL puts Carlton on notice
Sports.detail: Collingwood players reportedly informed the AFL that Elijah Hollands told them he was under the influence of alcohol during Carlton's clash with the Magpies, prompting an AFL request for Carlton's review of the incident.

Entertainment.headline: French film star Nathalie Baye dead at 77, says family
Entertainment.detail: The family of Nathalie Baye, a celebrated French actress, has announced her death at the age of 77. She was known for her roles in numerous films throughout her career.

Entertainment.headline: Madonna Debuted a New Song at Sabrina Carpenter's Coachella Set
Entertainment.detail: Madonna surprised fans by joining Sabrina Carpenter on stage at Coachella to debut a brand-new song. This unexpected collaboration generated significant buzz at the music festival.

Entertainment.headline: Wrexham's Hollywood takeover fuels economic boom in Welsh city
Entertainment.detail: The acquisition of Wrexham Football Club by Hollywood stars has spurred an economic boom in the Welsh city. The increased attention and investment have positively impacted the local economy.

Entertainment.headline: Actress Nadia Fares Dead at 57 ... Reportedly Found Floating Unconscious in Pool
Entertainment.detail: Nadia Fares, an actress, has died at the age of 57 after reportedly being found unconscious in a pool. The circumstances surrounding her death are still under investigation.

Entertainment.headline: Bhooth Bangla Box Office Collection Day 2: How much did Akshay Kumar's horror comedy earn on Saturday?
Entertainment.detail: Akshay Kumar's new horror-comedy film, 'Bhooth Bangla,' experienced a slight increase in earnings on its second day, collecting Rs 19 crore and bringing its total collection to Rs 35 crore in India. The film features Paresh Rawal, Rajpal Yadav, Tabu and others.

Crime & Justice.headline: Ukrainian police shoot and kill a gunman who killed at least 6 and took hostages in a supermarket
Crime & Justice.detail: A gunman who killed at least six people in Kyiv, Ukraine's capital, before taking hostages and barricading himself inside a supermarket on Saturday has been shot and killed by police, Ukrainian officials said. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy commented Saturday on the deadly shooting in Kyiv.

Crime & Justice.headline: California bear-suit luxury car scam ends in insurance fraud sentences for 3
Crime & Justice.detail: Three people have been sentenced in connection with an insurance fraud scheme involving staged collisions and damages to a Rolls-Royce while one of the individuals was wearing a bear costume. The scheme resulted in losses of $142,000.

Crime & Justice.headline: UK police investigate an arson attempt on a building once used by the Jewish community
Crime & Justice.detail: Police in the UK are investigating an arson attempt on a building that was previously used by the Jewish community. The incident is being investigated as a potential hate crime.

Crime & Justice.headline: Former chef facing 14 murder charges to plead guilty to aiding suicide
Crime & Justice.detail: A Canadian man who was a former chef and is facing 14 murder charges will plead guilty to aiding suicide, according to his lawyer. Abetting suicide carries a maximum sentence of 14 years, according to the Canadian Criminal Code.

Crime & Justice.headline: ICE agent faces assault charges
Crime & Justice.detail: An Immigration and Customs Enforcement agent from Temple Hills, Maryland, is facing assault charges for allegedly pointing a gun at two people in Minnesota. The agent's name is Gregory Morgan Jr.

Education.headline: CBSE Class 10 Results 2026 declared: KV (99.57%), JNV (99.42%) lead pass percentage; Girls outperform boys again.
Education.detail: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) has declared the Class 10 results for 2026, with Kendriya Vidyalayas and Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas leading in pass percentage, and girls outperforming boys.

Education.headline: Winning stroke of reinvention in 2026: CBSE's 2026–27 new curriculum.
Education.detail: The Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) is implementing a new curriculum for the 2026-27 academic year, signaling a reinvention of its educational approach.

Education.headline: UP Education Selection Commission enforces zero-tolerance policy with AI monitoring for exams.
Education.detail: The Uttar Pradesh Education Selection Commission is implementing a strict zero-tolerance policy during exams, utilizing AI monitoring to prevent cheating and ensure fairness.

Education.headline: My Vote Matters' campaign launched to help Indian students in UK register for local elections.
Education.detail: A campaign called 'My Vote Matters' has been launched to assist Indian students in the UK with registering for local elections, encouraging civic engagement.

Education.headline: JEE Mains Session 2 Result 2026 Live: Updates on expected cut-off, final answer key, AIR list.
Education.detail: Live updates on the Joint Entrance Examination (JEE) Mains Session 2 Result 2026, including information on the expected cut-off scores, the final answer key, and the All India Rank (AIR) list.

Lifestyle.headline: King Charles to honour Queen Elizabeth on 100th birthday with 'national address'
Lifestyle.detail: King Charles will record a tribute to Queen Elizabeth on what would have been her 100th birthday for broadcast on Tuesday, April 21.

Lifestyle.headline: 'The Drama' review: A wedding tale with a twist in the knot
Lifestyle.detail: A review of 'The Drama', a wedding tale with a twist, noting it as a compelling watch.

Lifestyle.headline: Trump signs order to accelerate access to psychedelic drug treatments
Lifestyle.detail: U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Saturday intended to speed up access to medical research and treatment based on psychedelic drugs such as ibogaine for conditions like PTSD.

Lifestyle.headline: Asian surnames have been the fastest-growing in the US, according to Census Bureau report
Lifestyle.detail: A Census Bureau report indicates that Asian surnames have experienced the most rapid growth in the United States.

Lifestyle.headline: Just what they wanted: fed-up New Yorkers revel in SantaCon fraud charge
Lifestyle.detail: New Yorkers are reacting to fraud charges related to SantaCon.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: How corporate welfare for hospitals is raising health care costs
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A drug discount program has fueled consolidation and incentivized prescribing more expensive medications, driving up health care costs.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Putin's wartime economy is contracting, even with high oil prices
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Despite high oil prices, Russia's wartime economy is showing signs of contraction, raising questions about its long-term sustainability.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: This tax would rain a wealth of unintended consequences
Opinion/Editorial.detail: A proposed tax is predicted to have several unforeseen and negative consequences, warranting a re-evaluation of its potential impact.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The modern world has made us ill-equipped
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Justin Klawans writes about how the modern world has made us ill-equipped.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Even the neocons have turned against wars in the Middle East
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Owen Jones discusses the changing attitudes of neoconservatives towards military interventions in the Middle East.

World.headline: Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again, Demands End to U.S. Naval Blockade
World.detail: The Navy of Iran's Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) announced the Strait of Hormuz is blocked and will remain so until the United States lifts its naval blockade, citing violations of a recent ceasefire agreement. The IRGC warned that any vessel approaching the strait would be considered cooperating with the enemy and targeted accordingly.

World.headline: Egypt Launches 27-Billion-USD Urban Development Project in New Cairo
World.detail: Egypt has initiated "The Spine" urban development project in New Cairo, with investments exceeding 1.4 trillion Egyptian pounds (about 27 billion U.S. dollars). The project, consisting of 165 residential, administrative, and hotel towers, is expected to create approximately 155,000 job opportunities and generate substantial tax returns.

World.headline: Russian Missiles and Drones Bombard Ukraine, Killing at Least 16
World.detail: A recent hours-long attack by Russian missiles and drones in Ukraine resulted in the deaths of at least 16 people. Ukraine's President Zelenskyy is reportedly pursuing more arms deals with allies to help counter Russia's invasion.

World.headline: 78 Filipinos Duped to Work in Scam Hubs Released in Myanmar
World.detail: Myanmar has released 78 Filipinos who were detained for immigration-related offences after being trafficked for employment in scam hubs in the country. This was announced by the Department of Foreign Affairs of the Philippines.

World.headline: Two US Army Soldiers Injured by Brown Bear During Training Exercise in Alaska
World.detail: Two US Army soldiers sustained injuries following an encounter with a brown bear during a training exercise in Alaska. The incident highlights the risks associated with military training in areas with active wildlife.

Zimbabwe.headline: ED puts trolls on notice
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa has warned against the misuse of social media to spread falsehoods and malicious content, emphasizing the need for responsible online behavior.

Zimbabwe.headline: Chivayo pledges US$3.6m windfall for MPs
Zimbabwe.detail: Controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo has pledged US$3.6 million to Members of Parliament, raising eyebrows and sparking debate about potential conflicts of interest.

Zimbabwe.headline: Harare pursues US$16.7m infrastructure loan deal
Zimbabwe.detail: The City of Harare is seeking a US$16.7 million loan to address critical infrastructure needs, including water and sanitation improvements.

Zimbabwe.headline: CCC pushes for inclusive government
Zimbabwe.detail: The Citizens Coalition for Change (CCC) is advocating for an inclusive government, calling for dialogue and collaboration among political parties to address the country's challenges.

Zimbabwe.headline: Fuel prices go down
Zimbabwe.detail: The Zimbabwe Energy Regulatory Authority (ZERA) has announced a reduction in fuel prices, offering some relief to consumers.
